answer_fields.scope_distinction: Adam: paper-wide recommended default for tested problems vs. Transformer: Adam setting used in its experiments; distinguishes a general default from an experimental setting.

claim_verdicts.reason: The passage directly states "Good default settings for the tested machine learning problems are α = 0.001, β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.999 and ε = 10^(−8)" as part of Algorithm 1, supporting every qualifier.
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The Adam paper (Kingma & Ba, 2014) specifies good default settings for the tested machine learning problems as β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.999 and ε = 10^(−8), presented as paper-wide recommended defaults accompanying Algorithm 1.

claim_verdicts.reason: The passage directly states "We used the Adam optimizer [20] with β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.98 and ε = 10^(−9)" in section 5.3 Optimizer, supporting the exact wording.
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The Transformer paper (Vaswani et al., 2017) reports using the Adam optimizer with β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.98 and ε = 10^(−9) in its optimizer-configuration description.

field_values.scope_distinction: Adam: paper-wide recommended default for tested problems vs. Transformer: Adam setting used in its experiments; distinguishes a general default from an experimental setting.

claim_verdicts.reason: The Adam passage directly supports "paper-wide recommended default for the tested machine learning problems." The Transformer passage supports that β2=0.98 is what "We used," indicating an experimental setting. Rewritten to avoid adding "chosen for training the Transformer model" framing not literally in the passage.
claim_verdicts.verified_text: The Adam paper's β2 = 0.999 is described as a paper-wide recommended default for the tested machine learning problems, whereas the Transformer paper reports β2 = 0.98 as the Adam setting it used in its experiments. Since one value is a general suggested default and the other is an experimental setting, they are not in direct conflict.

title: Resolve the apparent conflict about Adam's β2 by retrieving what the Adam paper specifies versus what the Transformer paper actually uses, and explain why both sources appear to disagree.
